title: getAffinity
description: When affinity is set to trusted or auto, it will capture the affinity  applied to be applied within the exception condition

title: hasCountryNearby
description:
**Boolean**: True when a country associated with the profile is present in the screening request field. 
**Integer**: True when the matched country has a distance in words of the defined integer value from the match. 
**Country ISO**: True when the named country is present in the screening request field.
**Integer, Country ISO**: True when the named country has a distance in words of the defined integer value from the match.

title: hasCityNearby
description:
**Boolean**: True when City name of the country associated with the profile is not present in the screening request field. 
**Integer**: True when the matched city and country has a distance in words of the defined integer value from the match. 
**Country ISO**: True when a city of the named country is present in the screening request field.

title: hasPobIn
description: Discards based on any place of birth ISO country listed, matching in the Metadata.

The  **=** (Equal To) (loose equals) operator returns a true result only if the value on the left is exactly the same as the value on the right. Ignores minor formatting differences  such as capitalisation, or special characters to ensure two values are functionally identical.

**Additional examples**
| Screened Name | Condition Name | Discarded |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| Vladimir Putin  | Vladimir Putin   | **true**   |
| vladimir putin    | Vladimir Putin   | **true**   |
| vládimir pÚtin   | Vladimir Putin   | **true**   |
| vládimir pÚtin**o**| Vladimir Putin   | false   |

The  **==** (Equal To) (equals) operator returns a true result only if the value on the left is exactly the same as the value on the right. Ignores  capitalisation to ensure two values are functionally identical.

Operator available for the *scopeFilter* options*

**Additional examples**
| Screened Name | Condition Name | Discarded |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| Vladimir Putin  | Vladimir Putin   | **true**   |
| **v**ladimir **p**utin    | Vladimir Putin   | **true**   |
| vl**á**dimir p**Ú**tin   | Vladimir Putin   | false   |
| vládimir pÚtin**o**| Vladimir Putin   | false   |

The  **===**  (Equal To)(Strict) operator returns a true result only if the value on the left is exactly the same as the value on the right and the data must be exactly the same down to the byte.

**Additional examples**
| Screened Name | Condition Name | Discarded |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| Vladimir Putin  | Vladimir Putin   | **true**   |
| **v**ladimir **p**utin    | Vladimir Putin   | false   |
| vl**á**dimir p**Ú**tin   | Vladimir Putin   | false   |
| vládimir pÚtin**o**| Vladimir Putin   | false   |

The  **!=** (not Equal To) operator returns a true result if the value on the left is different from the value on the right.

**Additional examples**
| Screened Name | Condition Name | Discarded |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| Vladimir Putin  | Vladimir Putin   | false   |
| vladimir putin    | Vladimir Putin   | false   |
| vládimir pÚtin   | Vladimir Putin   | false   |
| vládimir pÚtin**o**| Vladimir Putin   | **true**   |
The **>** (Greater Than) operator returns a  true result if the value on the left is numerically larger or chronologically later than the value on the right.
The  **>=** (Greater Than or Equal To) operator returns a true result if the value on the left is numerically larger than or exactly equal to the value on the right.
The **<**  (Less Than) operator returns a  true result if the value on the left is numerically smaller or chronologically earlier than the value on the right.
The  **<=**   (Less Than or Equal To) operator returns a  true  result if the value on the left is numerically smaller than or exactly equal to the value on the right.

title: and
description:
The **AND** operator returns a  true result only if all specified conditions are met simultaneously.

Operator available for the *scopeFilter* options

title: or
description: The **OR** operator returns a  true result if at least one of the specified conditions is met.

title: in
description:
The **IN** operator returns a true result if a value matches any item within a specified list or collection

Operator available for the *scopeFilter* options

title: not in
description: The **NOT IN** operator returns a true result if no value matches any item within a specified list or collection

title: contains
description:
The **CONTAINS** operator returns a true result if a specific value, string, or item is found within a larger set or text block.

Operator available for the *scopeFilter* options

title: containsAll
description: The **containsAll** operator returns a true result only if every single item from a specified list is present within the target set.

title: not contains
description:
The **NOT CONTAINS** operator returns a true result if a specific value, string, or item is missing from the target  set or text block.

Operator available for the *scopeFilter* options

title: startsWith
description: The **startsWith** operator returns a true  result if a text string begins with a specific sequence of characters.

title: not startsWith
description: The **not startsWith** operator returns a true result if a text string does not begins with a specific sequence of characters.

title: Request > Context Block
description:
Read the JSON request payload and extract the values from the JetScan/JetFlow **context** block that could be required to build the exception conditions. Every field inside the **context object ** can be targeted in a rule condition.

Data can be retrieved from:

| JSON | API | Request Body schema | DataPath Pattern |
| ------ | ------ | ------ | ------ |
| Request   | JetScan/JetFlow   | Context Block   | `context.<fieldName>`   |

title: Request > Record Block (JetFlow)
description:
Read the JSON request payload and extract the values from theJetFlow **records** block that could be required to build the exception conditions. Every field inside the **records object** can be targeted in a rule condition.

Data can be retrieved from:

| JSON | API | Request Body schema | DataPath Pattern |
| ------ | ------ | ------ | ------ |
| Request   | JetFlow   | Records Block   | `transaction.<fieldName>`   |

title: Request > Record Block (JetScan)
description:
Read the JSON request payload and extract the values from theJetFlow **records** block that could be required to build the exception conditions. Every field inside the **records object** can be targeted in a rule condition.

Data can be retrieved from:

| JSON | API | Request Body schema | DataPath Pattern |
| ------ | ------ | ------ | ------ |
| Request   | JetFlow   | Records Block   | `transaction.<fieldName>`   |

title: Request > MetaData Block (JetScan)
description:
Read the JSON request payload and extract the values from theJetFlow **records** block that could be required to build the exception conditions. Every field inside the **records object** can be targeted in a rule condition.

Data can be retrieved from:

| JSON | API | Request Body schema | DataPath Pattern |
| ------ | ------ | ------ | ------ |
| Request   | JetFlow   | Records Block   | `transaction.<fieldName>`   |

title: Response > Match Block
description:
Read the JSON request payload and extract the values from theJetFlow **records** block that could be required to build the exception conditions. Every field inside the **records object** can be targeted in a rule condition.

Data can be retrieved from:

| JSON | API | Request Body schema | DataPath Pattern |
| ------ | ------ | ------ | ------ |
| Request   | JetFlow   | Records Block   | `transaction.<fieldName>`   |

title: endsWith
description: The **endsWith** operator returns a true result if a text string finishes with a specific sequence of characters.

title: not endsWith
description: The **not endsWith** operator returns a true result if a text string does not finish with a specific sequence of characters.

title: exists
description: The **EXISTS** operator returns a "True" result if the specified  field is present in the request and contains any value (even if that value is zero or an empty string).

title: not exists
description: The **NOT EXISTS** operator returns a true result only if the specified  field is present in the request is completely missing or null.

title: Request > Record Block (JetScan)
description:
Read the JSON request payload and extract the values from the JetScan **records** block that could be required to build the exception conditions. Every field inside the **records object** can be targeted in a rule condition.

Data can be retrieved from:

| JSON | API | Request Body schema | DataPath Pattern |
| ------ | ------ | ------ | ------ |
| Request   | JetScan   | Records Block   | `request.<fieldName>`   |

title: Request > Record.Metadata Block (JetScan)
description:
Read the JSON request payload and extract the values from the JetScan **records** block that could be required to build the exception conditions. Every field inside the **records.metadata object** can be targeted in a rule condition.

Data can be retrieved from:

| JSON | API | Request Body schema | DataPath Pattern |
| ------ | ------ | ------ | ------ |
| Request   | JetScan   | Records Metadata Block   | `metadata.<fieldName>`   |

title: between
description:
The **BETWEEN** operator returns a true result if a value falls within a specific range, including both the start and end boundary values. Where the start value is lower than the end value

**Additional examples**
| Amount | StartNumber1 | EndNumber2 | Discarded |
| ------ | ------ | ------ | ------ |
| 35   | 30   | 60   | True   |
| 30   | 30   | 60   | True   |
| 29   | 30   | 60   | False   |
| 35   | 60   | 30   | False   |

title: equals
description:
The  **equals** (Equal To) (loose equals) operator returns a true result only if the value on the left is exactly the same as the value on the right. Ignores minor formatting differences  such as capitalisation, or special characters to ensure two values are functionally identical. Same behaviour as operator "="

**Additional examples**
| Screened Name | Condition Name | Discarded |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| Vladimir Putin  | Vladimir Putin   | true   |
| vladimir putin    | Vladimir Putin   | true   |
| vládimir pÚtin   | Vladimir Putin   | true   |
| vládimir pÚtin**o**| Vladimir Putin   | false   |

title: not equals
description:
The  **not equals** (not Equal To) operator returns a true result if the value on the left is different from the value on the right. Same behaviour as operator "!="

**Additional examples**
| Screened Name | Condition Name | Discarded |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| Vladimir Putin  | Vladimir Putin   | false   |
| vladimir putin    | Vladimir Putin   | false   |
| vládimir pÚtin   | Vladimir Putin   | false   |
| vládimir pÚtin**o**| Vladimir Putin   | true   |

title: Whitelisting Exception
description:
Whitelisting Exception 

| Header | Header |
| ------ | ------ |
| Function   | Add Info    |
| Value   | Add Info   |

- Function 
- Value

title: Technical Exceptions: Data (string)
description:
Technical Exception using data (string version) 
 
- Data - could have a link to the Data section
- Operator [Link to Operator](https://dashboard.dev.k8s.neterium.cloud/documentation/exceptions) - could have a link to the Operator secntion
- Value - string values.

title: Technical Exceptions: Data (block)
description: Technical Exception using data (block version)

title: Technical Exceptions: Function (string)
description:
Technical Exception using functions (string version) 
 
- Condition Functions 
- Operator
- Value

title: Technical Exceptions: Function (block)
description: Technical Exception using functions (block version).

title: extendedSource
description:
**Boolean**: Finds patterns up to 3 words before and after the screened name: `extendedSource = prefix(3words) + screened name + suffix(3words)`
**Integer**: Finds patterns the specified number of words before and after the screened name: `extendedSource = prefix(number of words before) + screened name + suffix(number of words after)`

title: originalSource_TO_TEST
description:
Finds patterns the specified number of words before and after the screened name and applies normalisation to characters when boolean set to false.
Equivalent to: `extendedSource(3, false)`

title: request.isIndividual
description: Discards a match is the request type is Individual

title: getMatchedWordCount_TO_TEST
description: Counts number of words matching between the listed name and the name in the screening request.

title: getMatchedCommonWordCount
description: Counts common words within the dictionary that are a part of the matched name.

title: getProfileWordCount_TO_TEST
description: Counts the number of words in the list profile.

title: getAllWordsMatched
description: Counts and checks if all words are matched.

title: getWordCount_TO_TEST
description: Counts the number of words of the screening request value.

title: latinRequest
description: Discards if request name is screened in Latin or Original Script Name (OSN, i.e. non-latin characters).

title: latinMatch
description: Discards if screened name is Latin or non-Latin (OSN). Example Request Name: Владимир Путин   Screened: Vladimir Putin ("latinMatch": "true") -has  been transliterated.

title: primaryWordCount
description: Counts  the number of words in "/profileSummary/name"

title: requestWordCount
description: Counts  the number of words in "/match/screened"

title: matchWordCount
description: Counts  the number of words in "/matches/match"

title: hasAddressIn
description:
Discards based on any ISO country listed, matching a metadata address.

This function may be used with the option, *scopeFilter*.

title: hasPlaceOfBirthIn
description:
Finds listed countries in place of birth.

This function may be used with the option, *scopeFilter*.
examples:
scopeFilter Example Format: 
```
"scopeFilter":"profile.hasPlaceOfBirthIn('fr','pt')"
```

title: hasAnyPlaceIn
description:
Discards based on any address or place of birth in the metadata matching one of the listed ISO countries.

This function may be used with the option, *scopeFilter*.

title: hasPlace_TO_TEST
description:
Discards based on Address Country or place of birth present in the Metadata.

This function may be used with the option, *scopeFilter*.

title: hit.getPlaceType
description:
For geo matches raised on the records.metadata.places  hits are return in an array:
`"/metadata/places[0]/country"`.
This function allows to discard matches as per place type available: address, pob, nationality, or other.

title: orderedMatches
description:
Discards matches for which the screened name is not in same order as the listed name.

For example, with Profile Listed Name: AL TIKRITI, SADDAM HUSSEIN

Screened names considered as ordered :
- AL TIKRITI SADDAM HUSSEIN
- SADDAM HUSSEIN AL TIKRITI
- SADDAM AL TIKRITI
- SADDAM XXXX HUSSEIN AL XXXX TIKRITI

Not considered ordered:
- HUSSEIN SADDAM AL TIKRITI
- AL HUSSEIN TIKRITI SADDAM

title: geo.hasSanctionClues
description:
Discard a match based on sanctionsClues being present in the API response information at: `results.matches.geoScope.sanctionClues`.
This information is only populated when geoScore is enabled: `"options": {"geoScore":true}`.

title: geo.hasSanctionCluesFor
description:
Discards a match based on the ISO country specified in the exception and countries detected in: `results.matches.geoScope.sanctionClues`.

This information is only populated when geoScore is enabled: `"options": {"geoScore":true}`.

title: profile.hasNationality
description:
Discards a match based on the Nationality present in the Profile.

This function may be used with the option, *scopeFilter*.

title: profile.isLinkedAs
description:
Discards match if certain links are contained in the DowJones RCA.
This function is only relevant for use with the dowjones collection.

Only allows operators:  `==` and `!=`

title: explainException_INTERNAL_DO_NOT_PUBLISH
description: Details provided for troubleshooting the application of exceptions.

title: CommonNameSize
description: TBC

title: Response > Matches Block
description:
Read the JSON response payload and extract the values from the JetScan/JetFlow **response** block that could be required to build the exception conditions. Every field inside the **results.matches object ** can be targeted in a rule condition.

Data can be retrieved from:

| JSON | API | Request Body schema | DataPath Pattern |
| ------ | ------ | ------ | ------ |
| Response   | JetScan/JetFlow   | Result Block   | `match.<fieldName>`   |
examples:
```
{
  "exceptions": [
    {
      "reference": "Example_Response_Data",
      "conditions": [
        " match.score <= '90' " 
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

**Additional examples**

| Example Field |  Example Condition |
| ------ | ------ |
| match   | " match.match == 'vladimir putin' "  |
| type   |  " match.type startsWith '/name' "  |
| tags   |  " match.profileSummary.tags in [ 'SAN'] "   |
| score   | " match.score <= '90' "   |
| profileSummary Name   | " match.profileSummary.name  == 'Smith, John' " |
| profileSummary Address   | " match.profileSummary.addresses.addresses  exists" |

title: Response > Variables
description:
Read the JSON response payload and extract the values from the JetScan/JetFlow **response** block and convert them into variable field.  Variable fields allow you to extract a value directly from the API response and inject it into a rule without hardcoding it. The pattern uses a ${<path/fieldName>} wrapper with a path expression pointing to the field you want to retrieve.

**example:** `${match/profileSummary/name}`

The path inside the brackets follows the structure of the response object, using / as a separator between levels. In the example above, match is the parent node and profileSummary/name is the field being extracted.

Data can be retrieved from:

| JSON | API | Request Body schema | DataPath Pattern |
| ------ | ------ | ------ | ------ |
| Response   | JetScan/JetFlow   | Result Block   | `${<path/fieldName>}`   |
